recentActivityGallery.alt: Waste clean-up at Chipata First Level Hospital
Community members participating in clean-up and tree planting
Tree planting activity at Chipata First Level Hospital

recentActivity.intro: In celebration of Earth Day, our team conducted a community initiative at Chipata First Level Hospital in partnership with the Lusaka City Council Health and Environment departments. The program focused on waste clean-up and tree planting to support environmental sustainability and promote a cleaner, healthier community.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.para1: Basava Social Welfare Foundation was established on the belief that true progress is achieved within a supportive and inclusive environment. As a social welfare organization, we are committed to creating an environment that empowers individuals, uplifts communities, and promotes equal opportunities for all. Our mission is to foster collective growth, encourage collaboration, and drive meaningful change that benefits society as a whole.
mission.para2: We are committed to supporting individuals and communities through education, humanitarian aid, and sustainable development initiatives that create long-term impact.

cta.title: Join Our Mission
cta.text: Together, we can create meaningful and lasting change

recentEvents.title: Founder's Birthday Celebration with Children at ZISE
paragraphs: Basava Social Welfare Foundation marked its Founder, Mr. Durga Basava's, birthday by celebrating with children at the Zambia Institute of Special Education in Lusaka.
The event featured cake cutting, food donation, tree planting and interactive activities aimed at spreading joy, promoting inclusion and supporting children with disabilities.

images.alt: Birthday celebration with children at Zambia Institute of Special Education
Tree planting during the Founder's birthday celebration

recentEvents.title: World Environment Day 2026 — Lusaka National Park
paragraphs: Basava Social Welfare Foundation reaffirmed its commitment to environmental sustainability by participating in the World Environment Day 2026 celebrations at Lusaka National Park.

Held under the theme "Accelerating Zambia's Climate Action for Green Growth and Sustainability," the event brought together government, civil society and development partners to promote environmental protection.

During the exhibition, the Foundation showcased its initiatives in environmental conservation, sustainable livelihoods and community development while encouraging tree planting, responsible waste management and environmental awareness. The Foundation also donated tree seedlings to the guest of honor, as part of its commitment to a greener Zambia.

images.alt: Basava Social Welfare Foundation at World Environment Day 2026, Lusaka National Park
Basava Social Welfare Foundation at World Environment Day 2026, Lusaka National Park

hero.title: Our Purpose
hero.subtitle: To create a positive impact in the lives of underprivileged communities through sustainable development initiatives and social responsibility.

team.name: Durga Rao Basava
team.bio: 10+ years of experience in engineering and managing projects with 20+ successful project deliveries. Specialized in structural design, execution, and leadership.

team.name: Sri Basava
team.bio: Financial Manager with strong expertise in budgeting, financial planning, and business analysis. Experienced in managing company finances, optimizing costs, and supporting strategic decision-making across multiple projects.

team.name: Charan Basava
team.bio: Chief Technical Officer with extensive experience in overseeing technical operations, system architecture, and project execution. Skilled in leading engineering teams, ensuring technical excellence, and aligning technology strategies with business goals.

team.name: Alekhya Basava
team.bio: Director and Vice Chairperson, supporting governance, community outreach, and strategic direction across the Foundation's initiatives.
